bibliographic_data.title: BayesFlow: A Probability Inference Framework for Meta-Agent Assisted Workflow Generation
bibliographic_data.abstract: Automatic workflow generation is the process of automatically synthesizing sequences of LLM calls, tool invocations, and post-processing steps for complex end-to-end tasks. Most prior methods cast this task as an optimization problem with limited theoretical grounding. We propose to cast workflow generation as Bayesian inference over a posterior distribution on workflows, and introduce Bayesian Workflow Generation (BWG), a sampling framework that builds workflows step-by-step using parallel look-ahead rollouts for importance weighting and a sequential in-loop refiner for pool-wide improvements. We prove that, without the refiner, the weighted empirical distribution converges to the target posterior. We instantiate BWG as BayesFlow, a training-free algorithm for workflow construction.

core_content.novelty_claim: The paper proposes casting automatic workflow generation as Bayesian inference over a posterior distribution on workflows and introduces BayesFlow, a training-free algorithm for workflow construction.
core_content.key_methodology: Bayesian Workflow Generation (BWG) uses parallel look-ahead rollouts for importance weighting and a sequential in-loop refiner for pool-wide improvements to build workflows step-by-step. The weighted empirical distribution converges to the target posterior without the refiner.
outcomes: Improved accuracy by up to 9 percentage points over SOTA workflow generation baselines
Established BWG as a principled upgrade to search-based workflow design
core_content.full_text_markdown: BayesFlow is an innovative framework for automatically generating workflows, which are sequences of Large Language Model (LLM) calls and post-processing steps. Unlike previous methods that treat the problem as optimization with limited theoretical grounding, BayesFlow uses Bayesian inference to sample from a posterior distribution over possible workflows. This approach naturally promotes diversity in generated workflows and provides rigorous theoretical guarantees. The framework, named BayesFlow, employs parallel look-ahead rollouts for importance weighting and sequential in-loop refinements for candidate improvement. It demonstrates consistent gains across various benchmarks and model families, improving accuracy by up to 9 percentage points over state-of-the-art workflow generation baselines on the math reasoning dataset and an average gain of up to 4.6% across all benchmarks.
